text.title: Monorepo with Turborepo Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every Monorepo with Turborepo application must enforce. These are HARD rules — implementation expedience does not override them.

rule.statement: Workspace declared via pnpm-workspace.yaml; pnpm install is the only canonical install path. npm/yarn workspaces are not used.
rule.rationale: Mixing package managers creates lockfile fights and inconsistent dependency resolution; one tool is the only sane default.

rule.statement: Every task is declared in turbo.json with explicit `dependsOn` edges. `turbo run build` walks the graph deterministically.
rule.rationale: Implicit ordering (run-everything-in-parallel) wastes the cache and creates races; explicit pipeline is the only auditable shape.

rule.statement: All workspace package.json files pin dependency versions exactly (no `^` or `~`). Renovate/dependabot proposes upgrades as PRs.
rule.rationale: Floating ranges produce 'works on my machine' regressions; pinning + bot upgrades is reproducible + still up-to-date.

rule.statement: Every task declares `outputs` in turbo.json so the cache knows what to store/restore. Tasks without outputs declared cannot use cache effectively.
rule.rationale: Without outputs, turbo can't know what to cache; cache hits become near-zero.

rule.statement: CI runs with TURBO_TOKEN + TURBO_TEAM set; cache hits are shared across workers and PRs. Without remote cache, CI repeats work per branch.
rule.rationale: Remote cache is the dominant CI speedup; without it, turbo's primary value is lost in CI.

rule.statement: package.json scripts at the root delegate to turbo (`"build": "turbo run build"`). Direct cross-package commands that bypass turbo are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Bypass scripts skip the pipeline + cache, producing inconsistent behaviour.

rule.statement: When preconditions in Applies-If hold and no Skip-If trigger applies, walk the procedure end-to-end and run the validator before declaring done.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory run leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree leaves must reference a rule id. The run leaf maps "preconditions met" to the canonical execution path.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the Applies-If preconditions are not met OR any Skip-If disqualifier holds.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory skip leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree leaves must reference a rule id. The skip leaf needs a real rule so non-applicable inputs route cleanly out instead of producing false-positive verdicts.
